entries.why: AI/word-processor output uses the single ellipsis glyph; a human typing in a plain field types three periods '...'. Strong tell in lyrics or chat.
AI reaches for the true em dash far more than a human typist, who types '-' or '--'. Em dash present in a plain-text field (lyrics box, comment) is a typography tell.
entries.example: I wanted to stay — but the road called

entries.why: AI uses a proper en dash for ranges ('2020–2024'); a human typist types a hyphen. Its presence signals generated/pasted text.
Smart/curly double quotes appear in AI and word-processor output; a human typing raw types straight quotes ". A near-proof tell in a plain-text field like a lyric box.
Smart apostrophes/single quotes ('don\u2019t') come from AI/word-processors; a raw typist types the straight apostrophe. Strong tell in lyrics and chat.
A non-breaking space is invisible but only appears in copied HTML / AI output — a human keyboard produces a normal space. Near-proof of paste.
The bullet character in running text signals AI/markdown list output rather than hand-typed prose.
AI uses arrow glyphs ('X → Y') where a human types '->' or writes it out. A typography tell.
Check/cross emoji-glyphs in text are an AI list/formatting habit; humans rarely type them inline.
AI writes dimensions with the true multiplication sign ('1920×1080'); a human types the letter 'x'.
AI uses prime glyphs for measurements where a typist uses straight ' and ".
Inline ™/®/© in casual text is a copy-paste / AI formatting habit rather than hand typing.
Double-asterisk bold is markdown; if it appears in a finished piece or a plain-text field the author pasted AI output without cleaning the formatting.
AI's signature bolded-term-then-colon list item; a strong structural + typography tell.
Leading # header syntax in body text or a lyric field is un-rendered markdown pasted straight from AI.
Backtick inline-code markdown surviving into prose/lyrics signals pasted AI/markdown output.
A markdown horizontal-rule line is un-rendered AI formatting pasted verbatim.
A template placeholder the author forgot to fill — proof the text came from an AI/template, not hand-written.

entries.term: no typos, doubled letters, or keyboard slips
entries.why: AI text is free of the transpositions, doubled letters, missed spaces and autocorrect artifacts a human typist produces over any real length. Flawlessness itself is a soft tell.
entries.example: A 300-word passage with zero typos, homophone errors, or stray keystrokes.

entries.why: AI is perfectly consistent (all curly or all straight); humans mix straight and curly quotes within one text, especially when editing.
entries.example: Every quote in the piece is curly; not one straight quote slipped in.

entries.term: exactly one space after every period
entries.why: AI never double-spaces after periods and never leaves a stray double space; spacing is machine-perfect.
entries.example: Uniform single spacing after every sentence, no exceptions.

entries.why: Every sentence starts capitalized and ends with punctuation; no lowercase-i, no missing end marks a casual typist leaves.
entries.example: No 'i' for 'I', every line properly capped and closed.

entries.why: AI capitalizes headings in Title Case ('How To Get Started'); casual human writing uses sentence case or lowercase.

entries.term: emoji as section bullets or headers
entries.why: AI decorates lists/sections with a leading emoji per item ('🚀 Fast', '💡 Smart') — a formatting habit rare in hand-typed prose.
AI separates every paragraph with an identical blank line; human drafts have irregular spacing.
entries.example: Every paragraph separated by exactly one blank line throughout.

entries.why: Contractions render with the curly apostrophe (don\u2019t, it\u2019s) rather than the straight one a raw typist produces.
